title: Distill audit artifacts to decisions and refs
goal: Implement #538 by making review and audit artifacts keep non-reconstructable judgment, compact refs, confidence, and reopen/promote triggers instead of copying source context.
Add guardrails so large audit records cannot become parallel history stores.
non_goals: Do not delete historical review artifacts without a distillation path.
Do not make GitHub or any provider mandatory for core review records.
Do not implement generated mirror or duplicate checker compression in this slice.

intent.outcome: review/audit records are compact decision records with reconstructable source refs
intent.constraints: repo/tool/language agnostic; preserve weak reconstruction refs; no broad archive cleanup
intent.latitude: may update review templates, review docs, structured inventory, checks, and focused tests
intent.escalation: stop if satisfying the issue requires deleting existing audit history instead of adding a distillation path

execution.next_step: archive #538 and continue the parent lane with #539
execution.proof: passed: structured inventory check, review retention unit tests, planning surfaces check, planning doctor, workspace CLI tests, ruff

invariants: historical review artifacts remain evidence/history, not ordinary startup input
source detail stays reconstructable from refs rather than copied into durable records
review schema work remains routed to #505 unless implemented directly

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: stop checking in reconstructable artifact residue

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: audit/review artifact retention can be checked as compact decision residue
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Large review/audit records now require retention metadata for source retention, distillation path, reconstructable refs, and intentionally omitted copied context; template and legacy large audits carry that metadata.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: None for #538; #539 and #540 remain in the parent lane.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Finish the current lane, then take a break.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: complete reconstructable-artifact compression lane in order
intent_interpretation.review guidance: verify review/audit artifacts cannot grow into copied source-history stores

execution_bounds.allowed paths: review artifact templates/docs/checks plus structured inventory and planning state
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run pytest tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py -q; uv run ruff check scripts tests src
execution_bounds.stop before touching: generated mirror files or planning checker duplicate copies

stop_conditions.stop when: review/audit retention rules and guardrails are encoded and tested

context_budget.context-shift triggers: before commit or after validation failure

invariants: Do not store provider-specific source history in core review retention rules.
Keep full source reconstruction behind refs, not copied status/title/history snapshots.
Leave schema implementation for #505 unless the minimal guard needs typed validation.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Review/audit artifacts may be checked in as distilled historical decisions, not as source-history mirrors.
open_questions_to_close: Which existing check should enforce large review artifact retention guardrails?
validation_commands: uv run ruff check scripts tests src
completion_criteria: Retention rule for review/audit records is encoded in a durable surface.
Size/count guardrail exists for many-classification review artifacts.
Distillation path for historical audit artifacts is documented or checked.
Future templates/checks discourage copied issue titles, status snapshots, and long command/source summaries.
#538 closes and parent lane routes to #539.

execution_run.what happened: Added compact-retention metadata to review templates and legacy large audit records, extended structured inventory guardrails to count review classifications, and added a check that large review/audit records declare source-retention and distillation metadata.
execution_run.scope touched: review templates, historical large review artifacts, structured inventory guardrails/checks/tests, planning state
execution_run.changed surfaces: review retention metadata and structured-file inventory checker
execution_run.validations run: uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run pytest tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py -q; uv run ruff check scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run ruff check src tests scripts; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q
execution_run.result for continuation: #538 complete; parent reconstructable-artifact-policy should route to #539 next.
execution_run.next step: archive plan, close #538, commit, then continue #539 later

finished_run_review.config compliance: used compact summary/proof and active execplan; preserved provider-agnostic source refs

proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: structured inventory check, focused tests, planning surfaces, planning doctor, workspace CLI tests, and ruff passed.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Distill audit artifacts to decisions and refs instead of copied source context.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: large audit records have compact retention/distillation metadata; future large review records are checked for that metadata; templates discourage copied source history
intent_satisfaction.unsolved intent passed to: #539 and #540 for generated mirror and duplicate payload residue

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve only context that is expensive to rediscover and necessary for safe continuation.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: decisions and refs over copied source context
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Do historical review artifacts stay compact and reconstructable?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #538 guards review/audit artifacts, but generated mirror justification and duplicate checker payload copies remain.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml routes the parent lane to #539; archive retains #538 proof.
closure_check.reopen trigger: large review/audit records again store copied source context without retention/distillation metadata

durable_residue.learned constraint: review/audit artifacts should retain judgment and compact refs, not copied reconstructable source context

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Review/audit artifacts now have a compact-retention rule and checked guardrail for large classification records.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: structured inventory check, focused unit tests, planning surfaces check, planning doctor, workspace CLI tests, and ruff passed
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: archive proof plus template/check updates; no Memory promotion needed
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): review templates and structured inventory checker updated

signals dismissed: No new dogfooding friction beyond #539/#540 surfaced during #538.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: bounded review of audit/review retention guardrails for #538
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: do not delete historical audit artifacts; keep tracker/provider agnostic; do not implement #539/#540 in this slice
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: specific template/check fields and focused tests needed to enforce compact retention
delegated_judgment.escalate when: satisfying the issue requires broad review schema implementation or historical artifact deletion
